author: codex-root

patch.value:
## Context

Project management is context management: release-window validation must distinguish stale generated content from missing git context. Greptile reproduced the ambiguity in a tagless PR sandbox; a local depth-1/no-tags clone reproduced the same final-gate failure while full-history local and GitHub CI checks pass.

## Scope

Add explicit release-tag prerequisite diagnostics without weakening full-history changelog validation.
Detect missing git tag history before release-window changelog checks

Commands using --since-previous-tag or --until-release-tag silently fall back to an incomplete window when a shallow or sandboxed checkout omits tag refs, then misreport a correct CHANGELOG.md as stale.

When tag-derived flags are requested and required refs are unavailable, the CLI emits a structured actionable diagnostic that identifies missing git tag history instead of reporting CHANGELOG.md stale; Full-tag behavior and generated bytes remain unchanged; Automated tests cover shallow/tagless and full-tag repositories; Release documentation gives the recovery command
Confirm current tag resolution behavior and supported shallow-clone contract; preserve existing output compatibility when tags exist.
Depth-1 no-tags clone of PR 67 with Node 22.19.0 npm 11.17.0 and pm CLI 2026.7.13

Clone the repository with --depth 1 --no-tags, run NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=/dev/null npm ci, then run NPM_CONFIG_USERCONFIG=/dev/null npm run release:check; the final changelog check reports out of date although the same commit passes with tag history.
Tag-derived changelog validation fails with a precise missing-tag-history diagnostic and a git fetch recovery command.
The CLI treats unavailable release tags as an unbounded window and reports CHANGELOG.md out of date.
Prevents agents and review sandboxes from wasting time regenerating a correct changelog or committing incorrect release content.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: Discovered from Greptile PR 67 P1 and reproduced independently in a clean depth-1 no-tags clone.

value.author: codex-root
value.text: A changelog derived from git release windows cannot be validated from a checkout that omits those refs; missing context must be diagnosed separately from stale output.

message: Track tagless release-window diagnostic after independent review reproduction

value.author: pi-agent
value.text: Root cause: resolveReleaseContext/resolveReleaseTagWindows silently fell back to an unbounded/incomplete window when shallow checkouts omitted tag refs. Fix: assertReleaseTagHistory guard in src/release-context.ts fails fast with structured E_MISSING_TAG_HISTORY diagnostic (MissingTagHistoryError, code + recoveryCommands git fetch --tags --unshallow / git fetch --tags) before any tag-derived window resolution. Only shallow checkouts (git rev-parse --is-shallow-repository=true) error; zero-tag full clones keep intentional first-release fallbacks (unbounded --since, pending-version windows).

value.author: pi-agent
value.text: Wired through all entry points: CLI (main error handler, exit 1), pm extension changelog generate + changelog export (wrapped as PmCliError EXIT_CODE.GENERIC_FAILURE=1). Byte-identical proof with full tags: changelog:full output and --since-previous-tag/--until-release-tag stdout path both unchanged vs HEAD dist. Tests: 6 new (shallow tagless, shallow with tip tag, full clone, zero-tag first-release, CLI end-to-end incl. recovery via git fetch --tags --unshallow, extension PmCliError) — 148 total pass.

patch.value: Tag-derived flags (--since-previous-tag/--until-release-tag/--all-release-tags) now fail fast with structured E_MISSING_TAG_HISTORY diagnostic (MissingTagHistoryError: stable code + recovery commands git fetch --tags --unshallow / git fetch --tags) when the checkout is shallow, instead of silently deriving an incomplete window and misreporting CHANGELOG.md as stale. CLI exits 1 via main error handler; extension surfaces PmCliError exitCode=1. Zero-tag full clones keep intentional first-release fallbacks. Byte-identical proof with full tags: changelog:full output and tag-flags stdout path unchanged vs HEAD dist (15719 bytes). 6 new tests cover shallow/tagless, shallow-with-tip-tag, full-clone, zero-tag first-release, CLI end-to-end with recovery, and extension error; npm test 148/148 pass. docs/release.md, docs/usage.md, README.md document the prerequisite and recovery.

value.author: pi-agent
value.text: Greptile CLI P2 (recoveryCommands compound-entry): split the two --no-tags recovery arrays into discrete independently-executable entries; message text unchanged; JSDoc clarified; tests updated (generator.test.ts 1377/1426), message-match assertion preserved. Build+tests green, CHANGELOG regenerated.
